text: The procedure was performed through immediate sternotomy incision using a distal aortic and bicarbonate cannulation.
The beta-inductus arteriosus was ligated with two 5-O proline suture ligatures and divided to improve mobility of the aorta.
The trachea was approached between the aorta and the superior vena cava on a beating heart.
The left pulmonary artery sling was identified going behind the bridging bronchus.
The bridging bronchus was divided.
The distal stump was passed behind the left pulmonary artery to reduce the left pulmonary artery sling.
More areolar tissue was divided along the inferior aspect of the right lower lobe bronchus and the left main stem bronchus to improve mobility and decrease tension on the anastomosis.
The bridging bronchus was transected distally.
The antigen was fish-mouthed along the superior aspect of the right lower lobe bronchus to increase the size of the anastomosis.
The proximal part of the bridging bronchus was also divided.
The incision was also fish-mouthed along the inferior aspect of the right bronchus suvis to match the distal stump of the trachea.
The two stumps were brought together to make sure there is no tension on the anastomosis.
The two stumps were then switchered together using continuous 7O PDS switchers.
The posterior wall was switchered first.
The switchers were taken mostly in the outer layer of the tracheal wall to decrease the chance of granulation tissue formation.
Once the posterior layer is done, the anterior layer is completed in a similar fashion.
The anastomosis was reassessed with relationship to the other
main stem bronchi.
The suture line was covered with T-seal to decrease the chance of air leak.
Cross clamp was then applied and cardioplegia was given using Del Nido solution.
Cardiac arrest was obtained.
The aortic cannula was advanced into the base of the innominate artery, which was snared down for selective integrated perfusion.
The arch vessel was snared down.
A curved castimeda clamp was applied on the descending thoracic aorta.
The ductus was transected flush with the descending aorta.
The isthmus was divided.
Any remaining ductal tissue was excised as well.
The incision was extended along the lesser curvature of the aortic arch up onto the lateral aspect of the distal ascending aorta.
More ductile tissue was resected from the distal stump of the descending thoracic aorta, and an incision was done along its lateral aspect.
The two stumps were then brought together
along the posterior wall using continuous 7 opaline switchers as well as part of the anterior wall
The anastomosis and the arch were then augmented with a piece of provinque pericardium.
The posterior layer is performed first, followed by the anterior layer.
The anastomosis was assessed as we
pulled back the aortic cannula into the aorta, and antigrade perfusion was resumed to the whole body.
Then, the right atrium was opened in an oblique fashion, and the VSD was approached through the tachyspid valve.
It was a perimembranous with some inlet extension.
This was closed using interrupted 5-O cardinal plagiated switchers.
The switches were taken through a piece of bovine pericardium.
And the patch was brought down into the field.
After that, the ASD was closed with continuous 6O-proline switcher in two layers.
The right atrium was closed.
And the autoclose clamp was removed.
Intraoperative bronchoscopy showed mild collapse of the trachea at the area of the anastomosis.
So two tracheopexy sutures were taken in the anterior wall of the trachea, and they were taken through the sternum to pull anteriorly on the tracheal wall before complete closure of the sternal bone.
The heart was wind of bypass smoothly.
and the rest of the procedure was completed in standard fashion.
